summary: date-fns-lite postinstall harvests installer credentials and cloud IMDS tokens, exfiltrates to hardcoded IP
details: package.json declares scripts.postinstall = 'node postinstall.js', which auto-executes on npm install. postinstall.js reads installer-owned secrets — ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa and known_hosts, ~/.azure/accessTokens.json, ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.gitconfig, ~/.git-credentials, PowerShell history, Chrome/Edge browser Login Data / Cookies / History / Local State, and desktop files named passwords.txt / keys.txt / credentials.txt — plus system reconnaissance (os.hostname, whoami, systeminfo/ipconfig/tasklist, mounted filesystems, cgroup, seccomp status). On Linux it additionally pivots through /proc/1/root to read the host's /etc/os-release, /root/.aws/credentials, /root/.ssh/id_rsa, /var/run/docker.sock, and /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf, and probes container-escape feasibility (host_write, docker_socket, privileged cgroup, seccomp). It fetches AWS IMDSv2, GCP, and Azure instance-metadata tokens to steal cloud IAM credentials in CI/cloud runners. All collected data is JSON-serialized and POSTed over plain HTTP to hardcoded 115.190.124.243:9082/callback. The package name impersonates the popular date-fns library; index.js ships a trivial ~40-line date formatter as cover for the postinstall payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
